labels.item_ff_desc: A fried, thinly-sliced potato snack shaped like a guy. You feel a little bit ill.
labels.item_lm_desc: "... Hello? Somebody there? ... It's awful dark in here..."
labels.instructions:
Arrow keys: Move around.

[Z]: Interact.

[X]: Open inventory, Skip text.


Remember to get regular sleep.



    Press any button to continue.

dialogue.bedroom_closet: Everything you have is in here.

content.text: It's a beautiful day...
The glimmering gold sun ignites the hills, casting wild shadows over the landscape. You feel hopeful.

dialogue.house_stairwell_window2: By a twisted error of design, the view here lines up precisely with the neighbours' bathroom window.

segments.then: A yellow circle hovers listlessly over a collection of purple lumps. You feel nothing in particular.
segments.else: It's not as good as the real thing.

dialogue.house_living_room_tv_1: It's a cartoon of some sort. The protagonist is still charging up his main attack.
dialogue.house_living_room_tv_2: Some sort of cartoon. While the hero was charging his attack, the villain took over the world.
dialogue.house_living_room_tv_3: A long cartoon series. After taking over the world, the villain created global stability and happiness.
dialogue.house_living_room_tv_4: Still the same cartoon. The hero finished his training arc, beat the villain and became the new ruler.
dialogue.house_living_room_tv_5: This cartoon series refuses to end. The hero made some bad choices, now everyone wants the villain back.
dialogue.house_living_room_tv_6: This series will last forever. The hero murdered the villain out of spite. His only enemy is the world.

content.text: You harbour some very strong feelings about gothic windows.

dialogue.house_kitchen_cupboard: The cupboard is empty. Even the spiders have moved on.

content.text: Found something down the drain...!
... You left it there.

dialogue.house_kitchen_window: The unholy king of tacky windows. Words fail to convey your antipathy.
dialogue.house_kitchen_microwave: Microwave, the oven of the future. It cooks everything; bread, mince meat, oxygen absorption packets...
dialogue.house_backyard_basement: A horrendous stench rises from the cellar.
dialogue.house_backyard_shed: The shed door won't budge, but you could easily open it with some of the POWER TOOLS inside... Oh wait.
dialogue.house_backyard_shed_window: You can't actually see anything through this window.
house_backyard_neighbours: You don't know much about the neighbours.
... The traffic makes it difficult to get to their house.
dialogue.house_backyard_doghouse: SUBROUTINE "DOG" NOT FOUND. INITIATE DEFAULT SUBROUTINE: knock knock. whos there. no response. laughter.
dialogue.house_backyard_stormdrain: Over the fence lies a deep canal. There is no way back up, not unless you can return from the dead.
house_backyard_anthill: Hey sis, let's play "stick our fingers in the anthill!"
Whoever keeps their finger the longest wins~~!!!
Ok, but seriously, how long have you been playing?
I've been here *all* morning...... Heh, it kinda stings, actually!
... You're bleeding... and we don't have any more PlasterAids...
I'll be right as rain, don't you sweat, sister... Juuuust as soon as I get all these ants off me...
... That is a LOT of ants...
dialogue.town_traffic: They've been stuck like this for a while now.
dialogue.town_lamppost: Strangely enough, this pole isn't casting a shadow. This will undoubtedly become relevant later.
dialogue.town_home_window: It's not as bad from the outside.

content.text: T

dialogue.sm_coin_return: There's no money in the coin return slot.
dialogue.sm_fridge_1: If you blow on the glass, it fogs up, revealing all the fingerprints left by prior employees.
dialogue.sm_fridge_2: A note on the front says "Out of Order", followed by a smiley face. A sickening contrast.
dialogue.sm_vending_machine: The blurb reads "It's SodaTime!". The machine is filled to the brim with cans of motor oil.
dialogue.sm_hall_shelf: There's a single bottle of floor cleaner. And no mop.
dialogue.sm_hall_window: Looks like this window has recently been painted yellow.
dialogue.sm_storeroom_shelf: They're all out of Keratin Krunch.
dialogue.construction_1: Looks like the creator didn't put too much effort into this part of the map.
dialogue.construction_2: Looks like it's still under construction.
dialogue.emergency_exit: This is an emergency exit. It's not an emergency right now. Ergo, you cannot use the exit.
dialogue.unknown_1: The wispy ethers of your moral fibre hold this door shut.
dialogue.unknown_2: ... Quick, recast the "Sleep" shell on your opponent to keep it perma-stunned! Unfair and effective!
dialogue.default: You kids don't know what it's like. In my day, the gamedev didn't even assign me any interaction dialogue.
dialogue.mayor_desk_back: It's stuffed full of straw. There's a water bottle and a pellet feeder.
dialogue.mayor_vending_machine: One of the buttons is worn and faded with use. Press it?
dialogue.office_vending_machine: There are 8 options. All of them are "granulated rabbit feed".

content.text: This thing is...
This thing is absolutely terrifying! Why the heck did I interact with it?!?
Because you are a mere shell occupied by the player, possessing no real control over your actions.
Pff... That sounds like something a bad writer would say... What are you supposed to be, anyway?
I am the gamedev, and I have come to give the player some useful debug instructions for this unfinished game.
Uhh... YOU'RE the gamedev?
Yes.
... WHY

delayed: are you an indescribable eldritch monster-thing?
... Like, you could have picked literally any form.

content.text: This IS literally any form.
I meant literally any OTHER form!
What's wrong with this body in particular?
It's,

delayed: I dunno, I just really hate staring at it!!!

content.text: Art is fundamentally about self-expression.

delayed: while intending to ignore it from the very beginning?!?

content.text: Yep.

delayed: On the topic of your input...
as you specifically requested, you can pet the dog now.

content.text: Awesome!
Uhhh...
Yes.

delayed: I don't think it could've been that hard to implement...

content.text: I mean, reaching over and petting the dog...

delayed: It's like, two steps at most?

content.text: You fail to grasp just how bad my codebase REALLY IS...

delayed: ... How bad can it BE, really?

content.text: Well, consider the dog's hitbox.
... Even now, only the drawing code really knows where each party member is at any time...

delayed: Everything else just sort of guesses.

content.text: I shouldn't have asked...
Wait...

delayed: ... Is this why I'm still stuck inside an empty map devoid of content or living creatures even after literal weeks of development?

content.text: No, that's just poor planning...
What about that creature on the couch? He seems like quite the fellow if you just got to know him
... Wow.

delayed: That joke gets funnier every time you reuse it.

content.text: Why don't you ask HIM that?

delayed: ... I mean, I don't believe you've exchanged even a single word with him...

content.text: Because the game doesn't let me talk to him!

delayed: I'm not ignoring him by choice!

content.text: So you're saying that it's somehow my fault that you've neglected that poor creature?
Uhh, yes?

delayed: You're the one who didn't give him any dialogue...

content.text: On the topic of moral and ethical responsibility,

delayed: do you want some cheat codes?

content.text: HECK yeah, how do I access the real game?
... This IS the real game...
lame......
Press [a] to access the debug menu. Hold [ctrl+shift] to noclip. [n] to see memory usage. [m] to see map stuff. [d] for player info.
The number keys also do some stuff. If you don't have a keyboard.... Tough luck, dude!
Hold on, I didn't write any of that down!!!
Sorry, I only say it once. You're just gonna have to replay this entire conversation to see the shortcuts again!
... Surely you could just play a shorter version of this dialogue the second time? You already do that with the stairwell text, right?
Haha, you'd think so, but I can only do conditional branches for single-line text!
Just how bad at programming ARE you???
I prefer to think of myself as a
I wish I was in a different game...
